applied_optimizations.name: Fused Triton kernel for bias + hardtanh + mish
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented fused_bias_act_kernel that combines bias addition, hardtanh clamping, and mish activation into a single Triton kernel. Uses manual tanh implementation (tl.math.exp) since tl.libdevice.tanh is unavailable on ROCm. GroupNorm uses PyTorch's optimized native implementation.

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton GroupNorm kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton implementation was 2x slower than PyTorch native (1.70ms vs 0.89ms) due to kernel launch overhead and two-pass algorithm

attempted_but_failed.name: Single-program-per-row GroupNorm
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correctness issues with sequential group processing

notes: Fused bias+hardtanh+mish Triton kernel achieves 0.18ms vs PyTorch's 0.61ms for separate ops
Triton GroupNorm was slower than PyTorch native - hybrid approach (Triton for elementwise, PyTorch for GroupNorm) is optimal
GEMM dominates at 90%+ and is already optimal via hipBLAS
Key ROCm Triton constraints: use tl.math.exp instead of tl.libdevice, manual tanh implementation, BLOCK_SIZE multiple of 64

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all produced no output - the agent keeps crashing or hanging on complex implementations. Providing near-complete code for the fused kernel to eliminate implementation guesswork. The
